title: Diocesan Whistleblower Policy (Appendix 19)
summary: This policy establishes the obligation of all diocesan responsible individuals to report improper financial conduct, policy violations, harassment, and other misconduct, and prohibits retaliation against good-faith reporters.
notes: 1) The document header reads 'Rev 8/14,' interpreted as August 2014; no precise day is stated, so the first of the month is assumed. 2) No explicit next-review date or review cadence is stated; next_review_date left null. 3) Category 'HR' was selected because the policy primarily governs employee/volunteer conduct, reporting obligations, anti-retaliation, and disciplinary consequences — aligning most closely with Human Resources / Personnel Records in the diocesan taxonomy — though it also has significant overlap with Finance (fraud/embezzlement) and Risk. A human reviewer may wish to cross-list it under Finance or Risk as well. 4) Owner role is tentatively assigned to the Chancellor, as this appendix-level policy touches canonical, civil, and criminal matters handled at the Pastoral Center level; however, the HR Director or CFO could equally be considered co-owners. 5) No retention period for the policy document itself is stated; under the diocesan schedule, governing policy documents are generally retained permanently ('Administrative Records documenting policy/procedure -> Permanent'), but this is an inference, not an explicit statement in the text. 6) The suggested chapter.section.item address (4.3.19) uses chapter 4 for HR/Personnel, section 3 for conduct policies, and item 19 to mirror the appendix number; this is a best-guess mapping and should be confirmed against the actual policy manual structure.
